page: Microsoft Azure DC16ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture is supported by 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 1229 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the CPU and memory performance of the DC16ds_v3 consistently in the average range. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is measured at 1897.99, while multi-core performance reaches 30344.22. Memory bandwidth tests show 1686.55 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 144.25 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The server exhibits strong performance in specific workloads, such as Geekbench image processing at 13913.0 and text generation for a small 135M LLM model at 310.57 tokens/sec. However, performance drops to a weak rating for large-scale workloads, as demonstrated by a prompt processing speed of 6.52 tokens/sec on a 70B LLM model.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and a substantial 1229 GB local storage allocation, the DC16ds_v3 offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive applications without the added cost of GPU hardware. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities for database operations, which scored 14720.12 on Passmark, and Redis SET operations, which achieved 31.87 million operations per second. This profile is suitable for relational databases, memory-cached web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure DC16ds_v3 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 1229 GB local disk. The instance lacks GPU accelerators and public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks generally fall in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 30791.92 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.87 million operations per second. It shows strong performance in image processing and small-scale LLM text generation, though it exhibits weak performance on larger 70B LLM prompt processing. This hardware configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC16ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and a 1229 GB local disk, delivering balanced performance for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, substantial local storage, and balanced performance for database and memory-intensive applications.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1229 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities
Strong performance in image processing and small-scale LLM text generation
Suitable for relational databases, caching layers, and software compilation
